description: UE5 Level Sequencer: cinematic sequences, camera cuts, tracks, bindings, triggering from gameplay, and Cine Camera

entries.title: Level Sequencer Overview
entries.description: Level Sequencer is UE5's cinematic and animation tool. It controls actors, cameras, animations, audio, and events on a timeline for cutscenes, intros, and scripted gameplay moments.
entries.whatItIs: ULevelSequence is a timeline-based editor that keyframes actor properties, triggers camera cuts, plays animations and audio, and fires events — all synchronized on a frame-accurate timeline. It replaces the legacy Matinee system.
whenToUse: Cutscenes and cinematics with camera work, actor movement, and dialogue
Game intros, outros, and scripted story moments
Scripted gameplay events that need precise timing (doors opening, explosions, lights changing)
Previsualization and machinima for trailers or storyboarding
Blending between gameplay camera and cinematic camera
whenNotToUse: Simple value interpolation during gameplay — use Timelines instead
Runtime-driven animations that depend on gameplay state — use AnimMontages or AnimBlueprints
Procedural or randomized events — Sequencer is authored, not procedural
Looping ambient animations on individual actors — use a Timeline or looping AnimSequence
commonMistakes: Not binding Sequencer tracks to the correct actors in the level — tracks show 'unbound' and nothing plays
Forgetting to set the Sequencer to 'Auto Play' or trigger it from code/Blueprint, so the cutscene never starts
Using Sequencer for gameplay interpolation that should be a Timeline — Sequencer is heavier and harder to control dynamically
Not adding a Camera Cuts track — without it, the player camera doesn't switch to the cinematic camera
Leaving the player controller in gameplay mode during a cutscene — the player can still move and shoot
keyPoints: Create: Content Browser > Cinematics > Level Sequence. Drag into the level to create a LevelSequenceActor.
Tracks: add actors, then add property tracks (Transform, Visibility, Material parameters, etc.)
Camera Cuts track: switches the active camera between CineCameraActors during playback
ACineCameraActor: camera with filmback settings (sensor size, focal length, aperture, focus)
Trigger from Blueprint: get the LevelSequenceActor reference, call Play(), Stop(), Pause()
Trigger from C++: ULevelSequencePlayer::CreateLevelSequencePlayer() or Get from the LevelSequenceActor
Events track: fire Blueprint/C++ events at specific frames in the sequence
Subsequences: embed one sequence inside another for modular cutscene assembly
